page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor. It features 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs with a dedicated allocation running at 2.8 GHz. The architecture includes 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled locally with 2850 GB of NVMe SSD instance store capacity, and network connectivity is supported by a 25 Gbps baseline bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 96246.73 and a PassMark CPU score of 49412.89. Memory performance is notable, achieving top-tier status in PassMark memory tests and a read-only cached small-block bandwidth of 6318.61 GB/sec. Data processing benchmarks show top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression at 36.5 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 635.79 GB/sec.

While prompt processing for a small 135M LLM model reaches a top-tier speed of 4218.03 tokens/sec, text generation for the same model drops to a poor tier of 31.56 tokens/sec. Additionally, the ARM64 architecture lacks x86-specific instruction sets like AVX or AMX, though it includes hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. Given the top-tier database operations score of 46541.54 and 136.39 million operations/sec in Redis SET benchmarks, this instance is suited for high-throughput key-value stores, static web serving, database hosting, and general-purpose data processing workloads requiring fast local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance powered by a 48-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of RAM, 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers strong multi-core CPU performance and top-tier memory bandwidth. Benchmarks highlight exceptional throughput in Redis SET operations at 136.39 million operations/sec and static web serving at 104.04 GB/sec. However, tradeoffs exist, such as poor text generation speeds for small language models and a lack of AVX or AMX instruction sets. This instance is optimized for database operations, high-throughput web serving, and data compression tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 instance with 48 Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier database and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 instance featuring 48 Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 2850 GB NVMe SSD for database workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance combining dedicated Graviton cores, high-capacity local NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 ARM64 cores at 2.8 GHz
Includes 192.0 GB of system memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in Redis SET operations and static web serving
Delivers 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Lacks AVX and AMX instruction sets, with poor text generation speeds on small LLM models
Best suited for database operations, web serving, and multi-threaded data compression
